title: Use Comparable types for Map keys
description: Map keys should implement Comparable to ensure predictable ordering and avoid runtime errors. Using non-comparable keys in sorted maps can lead to ClassCastException and unpredictable behavior, making code less robust and harder to maintain.
recommendation: Always use types that implement Comparable as keys in sorted maps. Refactor custom key classes to implement Comparable when needed.

noncompliant: Map<Object, String> map = new TreeMap<>(); // Object does not implement Comparable
compliant: Map<String, String> map = new TreeMap<>(); // String implements Comparable

checks.message: Always use types that implement Comparable as keys in sorted maps. Refactor custom key classes to implement Comparable when needed.
